zh.lifeSummary: 當Lambert II de GAND於1048年之後出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特時，他的父親Folkard van Gent8967歲，母親Landrade de Louvain8969歲。他於1064年與Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1118年在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lambert II de GAND于1048年之后出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特时，他的父亲Folkard van Gent8967岁，母亲Landrade de Louvain8969岁。他于1064年与Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1118年在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: GAND
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Wano, a short form of compound names based on the element wan ‘hope, expectation’. Compare Degan 2.

variants.name: Gain
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a cunning person, from Engaine, which is based on Old French engaigne ‘ingenuity, cunning, trickery’.

English: habitiational name from any of various places called after the Engain family, including Colne Engaine and Gaynes Park (Essex), d'Engaine's Farm (Cambridgeshire), and Aston Ingham (Hereford). Engaine (see 1 above) was the name of a powerful Norman family with lands in several counties. The original form of the surname has since died out, surviving as Gain and its variants. There has also been considerable confusion with Ingham .
French: nickname from a noun derivative of Old French gaainier ‘to obtain, gain, win’.

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lammert
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lamprecht
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Lambert II de GAND was born after 1048, in Gent, East Flanders, Belgium, his father, Folkard van Gent, was 8967 and his mother, Landrade de Louvain, was 8969. He married Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer in 1064. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1118, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lambert II de GAND est né après 1048, à Gent, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Folkard van Gent, avait 8967 ans et sa mère, Landrade de Louvain, avait 8969 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer en 1064. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1118, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lambert II de GAND nach 1048 in Gent, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Folkard van Gent 8967 und seine Mutter Landrade de Louvain 8969. Er hat 1064 Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1118 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lambert II de GAND è nato il dopo il 1048 nel Gent, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, il suo padre, Folkard van Gent, aveva 8967 anni e il suo madre, Landrade de Louvain, aveva 8969 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer nel 1064. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1118, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Lambert II de GANDが1048年以後にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンジェントで生まれた時，父Folkard van Gentは8967歳，母Landrade de Louvainは8969歳であった。1064年にMathilde Geyla de Saint Omerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1118年に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lambert II de GAND(이)가 1048년 이후 벨기에 스트-블라아더 젠트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Folkard van Gent(은)는 8967세였고, 그의 어머니 Landrade de Louvain(은)는 8969세였다. 그는 1064년 Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1118년 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lambert II de GAND nasceu após 1048, em Gent, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Folkard van Gent, tinha 8967 anos e sua mãe, Landrade de Louvain, tinha 8969 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer em 1064. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1118, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lambert II de GAND родился после 1048 г. в местности джент, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Folkard van Gent было 8967 лет, а матери, которую звали Landrade de Louvain, 8969. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer в 1064 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1118 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lambert II de GAND nació después de 1048, en Gent, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Folkard van Gent, tenía 8967 años y su mamá, Landrade de Louvain, tenía 8969 años. Se casó con Mathilde Geyla de Saint Omer en 1064. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1118, en su pueblo.
